patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/457

## Problem (never-block violation)
`pm health` can hang during the vectorization check with NO output until killed by an external timeout; same tracker returns immediately when vectorization is skipped. Reproduces with extensions disabled. Health is an agent-loop gating command — it must never hang silently.

## Proposed fix
Hard timeout + progress/heartbeat around the vectorization probe (bounded like the lock-wait work in 010bb349); on timeout, report vectorization check as timed-out with skip hint (--skip-vectors) instead of hanging; investigate the underlying blocking call (likely embedding provider probe without socket timeout — same family as the GH-455 item).

## Definition of done
pm health always returns within a bounded time with partial results on vectorization stall; timeout covered by spec with a hanging fake provider; docs note --skip-vectors.
GH-457: pm health hangs during vectorization check with no output (never-block violation)

Dogfood GH-457: pm health can hang silently in the vectorization check until externally killed (reproduces with extensions disabled); gating commands must never hang. Likely same provider-probe family as GH-455 but distinct symptom (hang vs noisy failure). Duplicate check: searched health hang — no existing item.
pm health always returns within a bounded time, reporting a timed-out vectorization check with --skip-vectors hint instead of hanging; spec with hanging fake provider; root blocking call identified
